nodes.context: Colombian drug cartel operating in northern Valle del Cauca, prominent in the 1990s-2000s.
Colombian drug cartel that fragmented, leading to the rise of the Norte del Valle Cartel.
Colombian drug cartel that fragmented, leading to the rise of the Norte del Valle Cartel.
Right-wing paramilitary organization classified as terrorist, employed by the cartel for protection.
Mexican drug cartel allied with the Norte del Valle Cartel.
Colombian drug cartel operating in Valle del Cauca, prominent in 1990s-2008.
Colombian drug trafficking paramilitary organization, started as Montoya's security force.
Colombian drug trafficking paramilitary organization formed by Wilber Varela around 2004.
Colombian paramilitary group; commander Carlos Mario Jiménez alias 'Macaco' involved with drug trafficking.

edges.evidence_quote: It rose to prominence during the 1990s, after the Cali and Medellín Cartels fragmented
edges.context: The Norte del Valle Cartel emerged after the fragmentation of the Cali Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: It rose to prominence during the 1990s, after the Cali and Medellín Cartels fragmented
edges.context: The Norte del Valle Cartel emerged after the fragmentation of the Medellín Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: The indictment alleges that the cartel members employed the services of the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia (AUC), a right-wing paramilitary organization internationally classified as terrorist, to protect the cartel's drug routes, its drug laboratories, and its members and associates.
edges.context: The Norte del Valle Cartel employed the AUC for protection of drug routes and labs.
The infobox lists the Sinaloa Cartel as an ally of the Norte del Valle Cartel.
The infobox lists the Cali Cartel as a rival of the Norte del Valle Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Los Machos was a Colombian drug trafficking paramilitary organization . The group started as Montoya's security force.
edges.context: Los Machos was the security force of Diego Montoya, a leader of the Norte del Valle Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Los Rastrojos is a Colombian drug trafficking paramilitary organization . The group was formed by Norte del Valle cartel capo Wilber Varela, alias 'Jabon' and one of his right-hand men, 'Diego Rastrojo', around 2004 when Varela fell out with fellow-capo Diego Leon Montoya, alias ' Don Diego '.
edges.context: Los Rastrojos was formed by Wilber Varela, a capo of the Norte del Valle Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Authorities now believe Wilber Varela's main captains Diego Perez Henao alias 'Diego Rastrojo' and Luis Enrique Calle alias 'Combatiente' ('Combatant') conspired with AUC commander Carlos Mario Jiménez alias 'Macaco' to murder Varela.
edges.context: Los Rastrojos members conspired with AUC commander Macaco to murder Wilber Varela.
